events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON file directly. The condition is a hot-work fire-watch omission, so I’m treating it as a non-compliant safety failure at the observed rough-complete stage.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.95,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"No fire watch was provided during hot work or for the required post-work monitoring period.\",
    \"Hot work controls are incomplete for a manufacturing floor with active production and quality gates.\",
    \"Area cannot be accepted until fire watch and permit-closeout requirements are verified.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop hot work and maintain fire watch until the hazard period has passed and the area is verified safe.\",
    \"Revalidate the hot work permit and assign a trained fire watch with appropriate extinguishing equipment.\",
    \"Inspect the hot work area and adjacent exposed areas for smoldering combustibles, sparks, slag, or heat transfer hazards.\",
    \"Document post-work fire watch completion before closing the quality gate.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Hot work requires active fire prevention controls, including fire watch when conditions warrant and continued observation after work to detect smoldering fires. Observing no fire watch during the work and post-work period is a direct safety control failure, so the rough-complete work is non-compliant and cannot pass inspection.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NFPA 51B - Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work: hot work permit and fire watch requirements\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart J - Welding and Cutting, including 1926.352 fire prevention requirements\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
node -e \"JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('/app/answer.json','utf8')); console.log('valid json')\""
→ /app
